headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
summary: The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo an international public health emergency. Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa with experts fearing the actual number may be much higher.

headline: Three killed in San Diego mosque shooting, gunmen dead
summary: Teenage gunmen opened fire at a San Diego mosque, killing three men including security guard Amin Abdullah, hailed as a hero. The shooters then took their own lives.

headline: Ukraine's attack on Moscow brings war closer to Russian civilians
summary: Recent Ukrainian drone attacks on Moscow signal a shift in the conflict, making the war feel less distant for ordinary Russians.

headline: Greenland's prime minister tells Trump's envoy self-determination is non-negotiable
summary: Greenland's leader firmly rejected any negotiations over the territory's sovereignty during a visit by a US envoy.

headline: Four Italian divers found dead in Maldives sea cave
summary: Bodies of four Italian divers have been recovered from a sea cave in the Maldives after a complex underwater rescue operation.

headline: Afghanistan crisis: Three in four people cannot meet basic needs
summary: A staggering humanitarian crisis continues in Afghanistan, with reports of fathers being forced to sell children to survive.

headline: Incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh suggests reduced global crisis role
summary: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh's comments about limiting the Fed's international crisis-fighting role have unsettled central banking peers who fear market stability risks.

headline: G20 trade ministers to meet in Wisconsin September 30-October 1
summary: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer will host G20 trade ministers to discuss forced labor, updating trade principles, and global overcapacity.

headline: Jackson Pollock painting sells for record $181 million at auction
summary: The Abstract Expressionist's 'Number 7A, 1948' has become his most expensive artwork ever sold at auction.

headline: Supreme Court rejects drug manufacturers' appeals over Medicare price negotiations
summary: The Supreme Court declined to hear appeals from pharmaceutical companies challenging the government's authority to negotiate Medicare drug prices.
